title: Shri Mehandipur Balaji Temple
intro: The Mehandipur Balaji Temple is one of the most prominent pilgrimage centers in Rajasthan. Dedicated to Lord Hanuman, this temple is revered not only for the deity’s presence but also for its unique spiritual atmosphere.
description:
Mehandipur Balaji Temple – Dausa, Rajasthan
Introduction
The Mehandipur Balaji Temple is one of the most prominent pilgrimage centers in Rajasthan. Dedicated to Lord Hanuman, this temple is revered not only for the deity’s presence but also for its unique spiritual atmosphere. Millions of devotees visit this temple from all over India, seeking divine blessings, protection, and peace.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Mehandipur Balaji Temple
 Location: Mehandipur, Dausa District, Rajasthan
 Presiding Deity: Lord Hanuman (Balaji form)
 Significance: Known for its deep-rooted faith and unique rituals.
History and Significance
The Mehandipur Balaji temple has a long-standing history and is deeply embedded in the cultural fabric of the region. It is believed that the temple holds immense spiritual power. The rituals and traditions performed here are distinct, and many devotees share experiences of spiritual healing and solace after visiting the temple.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Jaipur International Airport: This is the nearest major airport (approx. 100–110 km away). You can hire a taxi or take a bus from Jaipur to reach Mehandipur.
🚆 By Train:
 Bandikui Junction: This is the nearest railway station (approx. 35–40 km away). From Bandikui, you can easily find local transport to reach the temple.
 Dausa Railway Station: Another accessible option.
🛣️ By Road:
 Mehandipur is well-connected by road via the Jaipur-Agra National Highway. Buses and taxis are available from Jaipur, Dausa, and surrounding towns.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather remains pleasant, making it the best time for travel.
 Weekly Significance: Tuesdays and Saturdays are the most auspicious days for worship here, attracting the largest crowds.
Temple Timings
🕒 The temple generally remains open for devotees throughout the day.
(Note: It is advisable to check local schedules during special festivals as timings may vary.)
Nearby Attractions
 Abhaneri Stepwell (Chand Baori): A historic and architectural marvel located in the Dausa district.
 Harshat Mata Temple: An ancient temple complex known for its intricate carvings.
Dress Code
 As is standard for sacred sites, modest and traditional attire is recommended to respect the religious sanctity of the temple.
Facilities for Devotees
 Queue management and crowd control systems.
 Various Dharamshalas and guest houses in the vicinity for accommodation.
 Nearby local markets for offerings and Prasad.
Conclusion
Visiting Mehandipur Balaji is a unique experience that many devotees hold dear. The temple is a symbol of immense faith and spiritual devotion. Whether you are seeking peace or divine guidance, the atmosphere at Mehandipur Balaji is designed to help devotees connect deeply with their faith.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Mehandipur Balaji Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to the Balaji form of Lord Shri Hanuman.
faq.q2: Where is the Mehandipur Balaji Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Mehandipur, Dausa district, Rajasthan.
faq.q3: What is the Mehandipur Balaji Temple famous for?
faq.a3: It is famous for the devotion to Lord Hanuman, unique religious traditions, and the unwavering faith of the devotees.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit Mehandipur Balaji?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel.
faq.q5: Which days are considered special for Mehandipur Balaji?
faq.a5: Tuesdays and Saturdays are given special significance.
